message:
fix(engine): harden module resolver, gate hashing, and config comment parsing

Four interrelated correctness fixes across the engine's core subsystems:

- module_resolver: Fix parse_package_specifier to preserve trailing-slash
  semantics (e.g. "pkg/subpath/" must not collapse to ".") by checking
  specifier.ends_with('/') before defaulting the export key to ".".
  Remove premature empty-exports bailout that prevented fallback to
  legacy resolution when a package had zero export map entries.
  Replace iterator-based tail reconstruction with direct string slicing
  for scoped package specifiers to avoid allocating intermediate Vec.

- hardware_code_layout_gate: Replace Iterator::sum() with saturating_add
  fold in LayoutPolicy::total_code_bytes() to prevent silent overflow
  when the sum of region sizes exceeds u64::MAX — without this, a
  crafted policy with large region sizes could wrap around to a small
  total, bypassing size-based admission checks.

- native_addon_parity_gate: Include coverage_millionths in
  SupportSurfaceEntry hash and violation cohort in GateReceipt hash
  so that two entries differing only in coverage percentage (or two
  violations differing only in cohort) no longer collide in the
  deterministic audit trail.

- test262_release_gate: Fix strip_comment to track escaped-quote state
  (backslash inside double-quoted strings) so that a '#' character
  inside an escaped-quote sequence like "foo\"#bar" is not
  misinterpreted as the start of a comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
